channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: spaceX IPO — 115 replies — 460.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
spaceX IPO

Open thread • Replies: 115 • Updated: 2026-06-12 13:48 UTC

OP

are we ready for the biggest scam in history of histories?

ELON MUSK, YOU FAGGGOT

when will your ponzi scheme finally collapse, you fucking hobo

Early replies (first 3)

62351469
>>62351461
like 8 different brokers and crypto apps have shilled this shit to me fucking lmao

are they gonna rape retail instantly or let it pump for a few days before the raping?

62351500
It's actually going to be a repeat of the ICP ico isn't it?

62351509
>>62351469
>are they gonna rape retail instantly or let it pump for a few days before the raping?
supposedly this is entering indexes in 15 days

total scam of scams. can't believe this is fucking happening. everyone (ameriburgers) about to get raped

i'm not american but I would've voted for the faggot. but this...
it would've either been: continuation of woke lunacy or scams

hard times
>https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AEB6ibtdPZc

>are they gonna
the cult is real. it will pump and potentially crater with ai bust in 6-12 months (or god knows when or if)

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62356195
>>62351581
Writing memes on twitter is not a way to build your own country.
You gotta start with owning some land first. How much land he have? Zero.
Should've made his own army and conquered some part of Africa I don't know

62356200
I think I'm gonna buy some with my 401k. I dont care about a pump and dump. If SpaceX remains a successful company, it'll be around in 20 or 30 years and be way bigger than it is now. When they're doing routine missions to the moon and Mars and asteroids. This could be the biggest company in the history of the human race

62356211
>>62356035
>>62356055
Thanks

62356246
Lmaoing at all these first time stock buyers buying the most obvious scam in history. You're all going to lose all your money and I'm going to laugh so fucking hard.

62356251
>>62356246
Ok Thunderf00t, settle down

62356265
>>62356193
Kek im just memeing
Im an EUFrog im not gonna enter now.
In about 6 months.

62356276
>>62351469
theyll let it simmer for a bit to give you hope, just so you buy in a little more
THEN the raping happens>>62351469

62356285
>>62356251
Thunderfoot is based

62356298
>>62356246
Kek kek ! SpaceX is big scams ! Elon musk big scams, I am not buying this scams !

>gets allocated 0 shares

KEK I did not wanting this scams shares anyways ! SpaceX going to zero quickly ,,

62356303
>>62356246
Cope it's going to 200 dolla.

62356308
>>62355964
Don't worry saar , there will be many shares to buy when it crashes to $10 !

62356345
NON NEGOTIABLY looking out for the small guy

JUST THE TIP

62356358
y'all know his son is a tranny? not a janny. a tranny

62356377
>>62356358
>not a janny. a tranny
This is somehow an improvement.

62356388
>>62351461
I placed a limit order for IPO price of 135 on fidelity. Why hasnt it executed yet?

62356391
>>62356246
ok but what if it turns into another retard stock like TSLA?
shit just doesnt make sense anymore anon

62356406
>>62351461
Rooting for this one to explode on the ramp

62356439
>>62356406
Do you root for every IPO to fail or is it just because Elon musk is Nazi hitler?

62356452
>>62356439
No its because he's a zionist conman

62356463
>>62356200
>If SpaceX remains a successful company, it'll be around in 20 or 30 years and be way bigger than it is now. When they're doing routine missions to the moon and Mars and asteroids
I don’t know if you’re being sarcastic but aren’t other countries already ahead of the US in terms of sending shit to the moon? I won’t be around 20-30 years from now so idgaf either way but I would imagine that there will be a lot of competitors

62356480
why isn't it trading yet? shouldn't have started 10 minutes ago?

62356487
can't believe people are falling for this. guess they haven't read about how this company is doomed to failure.

62356492
>>62351469
Its gonna pump for 15 days, then it gets into indexes, and then it will crash hard

62356493
>>62356480
this
What's going on?

62356497
>>62356439
No it's just that everyone who buys for the asking price are either speculating or buying into magical thinking.

62356504
>>62356493
10-1030

62356541
>>62351469
Insiders shares are locked up with different unlocks from 90 days post IPO throughout the rest of the year. It will be a slow rug.

62356543
>>62356487
Lel

62356582
>>62351469
No one knows. My guess is that whenever it does happen, and I'm pretty convinced it will, it's gonna be very fast and very brutal.

62356589
>>62356298
youre going to lose whatever little amount of pesos you saved up to escape south america

item.title: No regrets selling BTC?? — 77 replies — 308.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
No regrets selling BTC??

Open thread • Replies: 77 • Updated: 2026-06-12 13:44 UTC

OP

Some talk about regretting it.

Anyone sold and still thinks it was the right move?

If you had the cash from that sale sitting in your account today, would you buy BTC again? Because I definitely would.

Early replies (first 3)

62339349
I sold my last bitcoin last year. Shitcoin is dead. AI is dead. Time to start acting like an african american.

62339372
>>62339349
Maybe regrets for buying? Should have bought chainlink

62339382
I rotated into XMR at like $105k, no regrets at all.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62349066
>>62345827
oldie bags but gold . Hodl

62349435
>>62346754
Onchain is the game

62349439
>>62345806
Depends on you lmao

62349795
>>62339328
If I had sold to pay off debt, buy a house, or improve my life, I wouldn't regret it. You can't eat unrealized gains.

62350085
>>62345787
go for shitcoins like memecoins on solana

62350154
>>62339328

Buy Polkadot

https://www.whitehouse.gov/presidential-actions/2025/03/establishment-of-the-strategic-bitcoin-reserve-and-united-states-digital-asset-stockpile/

62350283
>>62349435
Yeah. Going on-chain is one thing. Now let's see if the infrastructure can back it up. The public testnet on Aave V4 will speak for itself.

62350284
>>62350154
What happened to polkadot?

62350290
>>62345050

62350315
>>62350290
Ethereum is king

62351078
>>62350154
it's hurried from hell

62351090
>>62350283
Aave performs well as a DeFi platform, but the token itself is struggling.

62351106
>>62339883
Taking profits is never a mistake. Calling the end of crypto has historically been a much tougher bet.

62351114
>ETHEREUM

62351129
>>62345772
It's real until macro, ETFs, institutions, and changing market structure decide otherwise.

62351159
>>62339328
>Anyone sold and still thinks it was the right move
It's the definition of a bottom.

62352277
>>62351090
Hmmm, some people are here for the product, some are here for the chart. They're not always the same crowd.

62352286
>>62350315
Ethereum has certainly built one of the strongest ecosystems in crypto.

62354098
>>62351129
You have a point there

62354803
>>62350283
Maybe this is how people finally start exploring BTC in DeFi. Participation is still tiny. Imagine what happens if even 1% starts becoming 2%, then 5%.

62354820
>>62351106
I've actually earned more from memecoins than utility coins lately lol.

62354827
>>62339328
Anyone bullish on avalanche?

62355050
>>62339328
I sold when the Epstein files dropped. Feels good.

62355133
>>62354803
That’s the part a lot of people overlook. If TBVs actually work as intended, I think people are underestimating how much TVL native BTC use cases could pull in.

62355136
>>62355050
Time will tell whether it was the right call, but at least you acted according to your move.

62355617
>>62355133
It's crazy that DeFi security is still one of the biggest problems in the space.

62355631
>>62354827
Buy avax today and sell @ $100

62355632
>>62339417
Monero programmers, like the telegram founder, can be compromised
And monero is based on bitcoin. The entire crypto market exists because of all the money in bitcoin which is now backed by the US government.

62356269
>>62355617
At this point, thorough audits and multiple layers of review should be the standard, not a nice bonus anon.

62356538
>>62355631
What's your basis?

item.title: Some 16 year old told me he had worked a full week nonstop, so he was told he had to take the next five days off. — 75 replies — 255.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Some 16 year old told me he had worked a full week nonstop, so he was told he had to take the next five days off.

Open thread • Replies: 75 • Updated: 2026-06-12 13:32 UTC

OP

Some 16 year old told me he had worked a full week nonstop, so he was told he had to take the next five days off. I told him that was good.

I told him that most people will not make it in today’s economy when $150,000 is considered middle class. He replied that $150,000 is a lot of money. I said sure, but the average American salary is around $65,000.

Then I asked him what kind of work he did, and he said he did some low level blue collar welding. I told him blue collar work is terrible. He responded that welders can make over $100,000 a year.

I told him that blue collar work is for low-IQ, uneducated people and that the adults in those jobs are losers and people who messed up their lives. I also said blue collar work is fun when you’re a kid, but the adults doing it are there because they’re losers.

It turned out that three blue collar workers had been listening to our conversation and told the kid not to listen to me because I didn’t know what I was talking about.

I then told the kid that when he grows up, he will realize people look down on blue collar workers for a reason and that blue collar adults are not the kind of people he should want to know.

The blue collar workers called me ignorant, yet, from my perspective, they seemed to have chips on their shoulders and came across as unintelligent.

Why are blue- collar workers like this? Are they aware of how the world perceives them?

I wanted to talk numbers because no way most blue collar makes over $100k. Can someone fill me in?

Early replies (first 3)

62342722
>>62342700
Fuck off

62342735
>>62342700
I like this spam, because it counters the AI brainwashing (trades good, factory good, college bad, IT arrogant & overpaid or Indians, close the borders).

62342744
>>62342735
What?
>>62342722
Fuck you.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62344183
>>62344029
Yeah, still blue collar unfortunately.

62344186
>>62344036
Not really, a fourth worlder can come into usa and do the job.

62344274
>>62342735
I think it's funny

62344383
>>62344274
My threads are funny? Wtf do you mean? What is so funny about my threads?

62344425
fuck trade school. anyone smart should learn to vibe code an app that uses crypto.

62344611
>>62342770
Those people are ether:
1. Retired
2. Trust fund leeches
Now you might say, “nuh uh, they are not parasitic blood sucking invertebrates”
And to that I would say, yes they are.
Money has to be spread out even to the non contributing members and often times instead of focusing on growing their portion; they spend energy on stealing it from the producing members.
About 1/4 of every family is productive.
About 1/2 are ambitious (not profitable)
And 4/4 are living luxuriously
That formula produces 1 producer, 2 layabouts and 1 thief; on average, in every generation.
Basically somebody has to be making the money wether it’s a company ran by the one ambitious and smart family member or my tax dollars in which case you are only advocating for leeching off of somebody and floating the idea it should be me and most people on this board.
So please fuck off and kys, immediately
Please

62344720
You know biz is in dire straits when it's gone the way of /ck/ with so many bot threads to generate a false sense of activity.... Remember to sage these threads

62344811
>>62342770
How poorfag coded are you people that you think just maxing out a couple of credit cards is a lot of money? Like 10-20k might sound like " a lot of money", but even if you aren't rich thats like a year or two of savings, how is that shit going to last you your entire life?

62344833
You won't make it as a blue collar wagie but that doesn't mean its a dead end

Open your own business and build, in the end youre the boss of blue collar wagies and make more than 100k, blue collar work is high in demand and always will, someone has to build the physical shit and it aont be KI

62346901
>>62342700
never change, anon. I enjoy your little gay larps. i'm your fan, you're funny as fuck, I wish we had a biz party somewhere, you'd be the soul of the party unironically speaking

62346960
>>62346901
What larp?

62346974
>>62344611
>trust fund leeches.
Kek. Seething working class.
>>62344811
> How poorfag coded are you people that you think just maxing out a couple of credit cards is a lot of money
Not poor but maxing out one credit card is a lot of money.

62349408
>>62342798
bro you're onto something.
Fake accounts FLOOD the internet. Especially kinky shit like fetlife and the kink subreddits - they will have the exact same contact for totally different account and profiles.
I found 2 accounts that had each other as friends.. they had similar profiles, said they "knew each other online" and 1 used a keyword that the other one was talking to me and using..
there is a MASSIVE psyop happening.

62349422
>>62342700
OP, you clearly haven't heard this enough, you're a faggot.

62351731
>>62346960
everything that you post (on an almost daily basis) that never actually happened outside your autistic mongoloid mind, champ. I love these stories about the "middle class" lol. Keep up the good work soldier. thanks for the laughs (unironically speaking)

62354799
>>62342905
Elliot only drove a BMW because his dad had some cushy Hollywood job working on films like Hunger Games, etc. Pretty sure he wrote something in his manifesto seething about seeing some guy driving a shitbox and still having a beautiful girl in the passenger seat too.

62354802
>>62342834
ha, i was thinking this read exactly like that post

62354821
>>62342828
When they're writing long form fiction they get a pass.

62355262
>>62342700
Electrical contractor here. I live closer to the beach than you do. It’s kind of funny, most skilled tradesmen look down on people like yourself. We feel sorry for you because in the grand scheme of things - you’re worthless. Unable to build or fix anything. Totally dependent on others. Kinda pathetic

62355284
>>62355262
The mind of a blue collar is truly pathetic.

62355301
>>62342770
I haven’t worked in 1,5 months and i can already feel myself wanting to just become passive and not really being impressed or motivated by anything (brain getting fried by dopamine and serotonine). You have to be exactly the kind of ultra degenerate basement dweller who spends all his time in front of screens if you think that not working at all is good. Humans need the struggle in order to feel alive.

62355307
>>62355301
You’re pathetic. Since a little kid, all the classmates of my have been retired.

62355313
>>62355301
> You have to be exactly the kind of ultra degenerate basement dweller who spends all his time in front of screens if you think that not working at all is good. Humans need the struggle in order to feel alive.
You’re probably just poor. People making a top 10% income from investments tend not to work.

62355322
>>62355301
>1.5 months
You don’t even have a non working lifestyle built yet.

62355326
>>62355307
This may be the funniest claim I've seen you make. Absolutely nobody from your school does any kind of work at all? Most people do find things to keep themselves occupied with, beyond mere shitposting on an anonymous uzbekistani finger painting notification board.

62355338
>>62355326
Maybe we grew up in different classes.
On one hand, /biz/ loves to larp as rich and successful and on the other hand, loves to larp as muh productive and I don’t like doing nothing.
Fucking jokes.
I hear people say what you say all the time. Yet look at retired people, I don’t see you saying shit to them. Yet when it is someone young and not working, suddenly they are “unproductive.” Even though these people are clearly rich.

62355342
>>62355307
>>62355313
>>62355322
Kek did i trigger you too much?

62355360
>>62355342
I have never in my life looked at someone not working and thought anything bad.
Growing up, my dad retired when I was like 5. By 10, the parents of all my school friends were retired long ago.
By past college, the differences between the haves and have nots became noticeable because college is where you truly see class differences because high school and under tend to mainly kids from your neighborhood.
You cannot imagine life without work yet kids that grew up like me were surrounded by adults that don’t work. It is the normal. People like me just carry on the lifestyle.
People from successful families don’t break their back.

62355375
>>62355301
Depends what you mean by work. But any occupation today is not a good use of time. Like work as in what? Something you need to get a college degree for? White collar?
Or something blue collar? That’s even dumber.
Starting your own business? Okay sure, if you want to start it as a hobby..?
I don’t know. People build lifestyles when they don’t need to work to live.
Some travel, some have many social gatherings
>Humans need the struggle in order to feel alive.
Oh yeah, the bottom 90% definitely feel alive struggling lol.
Struggling for what? Lmao.

62355404
>>62355301
Sometimes I see the kids in my neighborhood. This isnt an ultra rich 100m a year neighborhood or millions a month neighborhood but in 10-20 years when these kids grow up, it would be a waste of time for them to get a “job.”
Get it?

item.title: You MUST post in this thread if you are waging rn — 60 replies — 240.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
You MUST post in this thread if you are waging rn

Open thread • Replies: 60 • Updated: 2026-06-12 13:47 UTC

OP

wage

Early replies (first 3)

62351330
I'm currently hiding in the bathroom

62351353
>>62351325
Im not desu

62351358
>>62351325
i hate being a wagie so fucking much

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62353572
>>62351325
Please end my suffering.

62354318
>>62351325
I'm not at work, but no matter how hard I try, my brain won't let me focus more than 20% without thinking about work and my body won't let me do anything productive. Should be recovered by 9am tomorrow though.

62354357
27 an hour here..

62354366
I wish I was waging. Life is boring with no reason to set an alarm every morning.

62354378
>>62351325
I make $68.50 an hour with only $35 of that being taxed and I feel like I'm a literal slave

62354379
any fellow walmart wagie here?

62354392
>>62354379
I feel like if you did nothing all day at walmart, the store would still be the same.
Every time I go there the cashiers and associates look like walking dead. Like theyve had their souls ripped out. A lot of merch is unorganized and shoved in random places. Theres people screaming far off into the other side of the store.
They just leave the (fake) jewelry inside a case and you have to walk 5 minutes to find an associate to open it for you... The deli has the loudest, angriest black woman who ever lived working behind it arguing with a mexican lady who barely understands english...

You must have abandoned your soul long ago to survive that place.

62354413
>>62351325
26/hr

>t. Warehousefag

62354419
>>62354379
I have always wanted to work at wal mart.

62354430
>>62354366
ok so get a job nigger

62354461
>>62354392
its not that bad for me at least, i quite enjoy it desu.

im the online picker guy so theres not much customer interaction and if the picking is done for the day (sometimes, like 30% chance) its very chill as you gotta be very close to your office for when the customer shows up

so im just stocking easy shit or chilling in the back, if i was front end tho id probably have my soul dead too

>>62354419
better do it soon before AI replaces walmart wagies, dont do cashier unless you like that position for some reason

62354589
>>62351325
I'm a third world wagecuck that spends his income buying 5 ounces of silver (or 1/10 ounce of gold) each month

62354608
Wagies, how do you find the time and energy to work on your projects, education, hobbies etc after the day is over?

62354635
>>62351325
I don't want a job where I need to talk to customers all day (some are fine but not all fucking day like a call centre or a shop) and no physical labor. What do?

62354643
>>62354461
Not cashier but something on the floor. That and I also wanted to work a MacDonalds kitchen job too.

62354655
>>62354461
I want to be a store wagie. Tired of insane hours

>t. Wmt trucker

62354661
>>62354608
one of the trick is to live very near your workplace, im lucky as my apartment is only 10 mins max of travel, sometimes i go home and eat on my lunch breaks

this is a bit luck based, but a job where you can slack off if your main shit is done. my boss dont care what I do desu as long as his online pickup stats are good so sometimes I do my shit during work time

other jobs i can think of is something like subway where theres not much to do during downtime and you're often lowly staffed like 2-3 people max so its very relaxed. i dont think you could chill during downtime in something like mcdonalds for example

8 hours of sleep/8 hours of work and usually around 6 hours of free time for me after showering cooking etc

62354675
>>62354655
dam my condolences

62354681
I'm sitting on the toilet now while waging.

I always take multiple shits per day while being paid for it.

62354699
>>62354675
It’s such a joke. Trucking is a nightmare, no wonder jeets do it

62354715
>>62352824
I can't or I get in biiiiiiig trouble

62354716
>>62351325
I being paid to sleep in a hotel because tradies keep fucking up the infrastructure for our machine

62354761
>>62354699
Can't believe how many jets have jumped into the industry. I feel like the average normie isn't aware of their presence in that sector.

62354806
>>62354761
People have no idea how much danger they are in on the highway because of the shitjeets. It’s an existential threats to everybody

62354992
>>62354661
Idk maybe I'm weak but I need to "decompress" after work

62355007
>>62354992
> Idk maybe I'm weak but I need to "decompress" after work
Same. It is so draining.

62356088
I have alot of down time at my workplace too
big issue for me is a 2 hour commute every day but I put up with it since my rent is cheap

62356156
>>62353364
Ash Grove. In that pic I was troubleshooting a truck loading issue. I discovered that our loading system was haunting loading due to some shutdown condition for a split second and tgen deciding to be happy again, most likely fluctuations in the scale wait from the truck or scale shaking while loading. The momentary blip breaks a logic latch forcing a retard of the loading process each time. Now I get to find out how to use timer off delays in unity pro and proworx32 to filter out that momentary blip. Every day is a new problem I have to figure out and I enjoy that.

62356177
>>62356156
I like how my phone knows that I call people retards on 4chan so often it autocorrect a typo of reload to retard

62356574
I'm shitting

item.title: the fucking jeets my managers hired have fucked me over. — 50 replies — 200.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
the fucking jeets my managers hired have fucked me over.

Open thread • Replies: 50 • Updated: 2026-06-12 13:46 UTC

OP

the fucking jeets my managers hired have fucked me over. tamil fucks. they didnt put any data loggers as stipulated by sop into the fucking pallets they were packing and all the data loggers are fucking missing holy fuck
COO scheduled a meeting with me along with 3 different VP im so fucked holy fucking shit. 72 pallets of insulin is fucked. I only became head of APAC logistics 2 years ago fucks sake. I know they want to get rid of me, opportune. FUCK THIS.

Early replies (first 3)

62353920
>>62353896
you were too trusting. This is your mistake when you know they're trying to get rid of you. That's when you should have stepped up your game or started looking for a new job.

62353921
Im so sick of this. How is it my fault when they remove all my budget for manpower

62353940
>>62353896
they only know one type of log and its not the clean kind.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62354164
>>62353896
How come the upper management and boomers are jewet enabling blue pilled cucks but the people who work for them and keep things running are all redpilled 4chan anons? How come we're not the ones in charge of these companies?

62354166
>>62354156
>How can i check on them if im not on site?
>>62354114
>have someone sending you pictures of things as they get done.

the more I read your posts the more I think I'd probably fire you too.

62354172
>>62354164
That would require social skills

62354174
>>62354166
Thats lower managements job you retard. Im head of logistics for the entirety of asia pacific. I cant have 70 different warehouses sending me pics of pallets and shit fuckface

62354175
>>62354114
I think this is right. OPs job isn't like some regular wagie in retail. He's hired to solve problems. He is allowed to think outside of the box as long as he is following all guidelines and requests. Hate to be that guy but it really does show the responsibilities people end up having at big boy jobs

62354176
>>62354164
>How come we're not the ones in charge of these companies?
because you're idiots.

generally speaking the smartest people are the most liberal. Though rich people tend to swing clear back around to the right because taxes.

62354178
>>62354174
>I cant have 70 different warehouses sending me pics of pallets and shit fuckface
then they should be sending them to lower management and you should be checking in with them.

someone clearly fucked up and since it was your system that failed, you're going to get fired. Figure out a better system so stuff like that can't happen

62354186
>>62354101
>>62354164
They are on /pol/ being hypocrites nigger posting at the end of the day. Hardly here on /biz/ to have intelligent conversations.

62354187
>>62354176
>generally speaking the smartest people are the most liberal. Which is why we all think the same way, went to the same universities and hold identical opinions

62354223
>>62354187
are there any illiberal colleges?

62354238
>>62354223
Ones without huge humanities sections

62354322
Well keep us updated

62354335
>>62354101
Good riddance, fucks like you fleece US jobs.

62354336
>>62354176
I was very surprised when I heard Magnus Carlsen wasn't a conservative and Vitaliks political viewpoints always throw me for a loop

62354359
>>62354335
These warehouses are in asia you retard

62354363
>>62354223
Go to a red state and hope for the best.

62354369
I haven’t had many encounters with jeets yet, though my employer has outsourced a ton of work to one of these agencies (v.globant). they’ve also outsourced a ton to south america over the past year. last week I opened a jira ticket for an issue with an internal tool that was acknowledged and being worked on by one of the remaining whites. the next day one of the argentinians hijacked my ticket and edited it to be about an entirely separate bug for a different application. I have to assume he somehow doesn’t know how to create a jira ticket so just edited an existing one.

62354374
>>62353896
Just claim that youre a masculine presenting lesbian transvestite.

62354498
That’s just a log, you can fake a log with computer generated one and the boss can’t even tell it is falsified lmao you are naive little white bois being too nice.

62354858
>>62354174
it's your responsibility to ensure a positive outcome. the outcome is negative, therefore you failed. it's shrimple

62354952
>>62353921
As early as possible in this meeting, tell them it's a good thing you're here to being this to their attention. Otherwise this is the future of the company on the current trajectory. Convince them that this incident was a godsend. To fail brilliantly is to offer hope. To fail in darkness and in silence... That is certain doom.
That is why you are going to ask for TWICE the amount of funding you origin thought you needed.
Insurance will cover the jeet mistakes and the lesson here is invaluable. Congrats. You just promoted yourself to C suite. You are a true alchemist.

62354959
>>62354359
That’s kind of interesting.
Were you moving product from India itself?

62355003
>>62354186
Ya got me

62355018
>>62353896
JEETed

62355042
I’m so glad I started NEETING around 2014. Imagine if I had put in the effort to have a career and kids just to get rug pulled. I would KMS. Now I can enjoy the destruction.

62355166
>>62353896
Lemme tell you something bud, Brown people ain't shit. Everyone knows it. There was a time when everyone wanted to be in charge, to boss everyone around.. now I dont know anyone who wants a leadership position in my line of work. Nobody wants to be the poor unfortunate soul who has to tard-wrangle all these dark skinned whiny bitches. None of them give a fuck. Theyll walk right past something fucked up without ever mentioning it to the boss and it gets covered up and someone like me has to fix it. The people like me realize we're being slowly replaced and if its this bad when its like 50% white, I cant imagine when its my turn to be in charge and its 30% white. The blacks form little "mini-unions" where they all fuck off and if you have a problem with one of them fucking off then you have a problem with all of them fucking off, in my experience blacks are the laziest of all. I dont ever see jeets because theyre apparently even lazier than blacks because they dont take work jobs, they take delivery jobs where you get to sit down all day. I worked with one jew who was actually a really cool guy and a very good friend but one day he told me his sinagogue put him in a secret society called the freemasons and he wasnt even sure why he was in it. I dont like that. I dont like that jews are placed in secret societies in our countries when they come of age. I dont see asians at all in these positions so really they just need to stay in their own countries. Thats cool if they all want to be scientists and never build anything but they can go back to china and Japan and do that shit.

You dont want to be in charge of brown people and "me me me" people. You dont want to do the work of 6 fucking people, fuck surveillance, fuck always staying on these peoples ass, we need white people. We need more white people and less brown people.

62356510
>>62353896
Haha

62356526
>>62353940
Kek

62356539
>>62354101
Kek again
get fucked traitor

62356554
>>62355166
No one is reading all this in the age of AI slop

Open thread • Replies: 36 • Updated: 2026-06-12 13:50 UTC

OP

62354945

https://www.icmagroup.org/assets/documents/Regulatory/Repo/ICMA-report-DLT-and-repo-part-I-June-2026-040626.pdf

62354971

i have sold much more of my stack than i wanted assuming that price would react by now

62354976
>>62354943

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62355053
>>62355051
Oh, I didn't realise you were a fuddie faggot. Like 3 years

62355059
>Fucking cocksucking bitch mader benchod

62355125
>>62354943
It's absolutely insane how (((they))) managed to suck all the hype out of Swift through years of ruthless Bitcoin dumps on all news.

62355130
>>62355034
Link has been needed since day 1 of mainnet back in 2019.

kys spammy

62355139
>>62355130
Friendly fire

62355143
>>62355139
absolutely not

62355153
Swift basically the guy who tried to put a motor on his horse carriage

62355562
shut the fuck up and just take the goddamn covid vaccine you fucking incels have gone too far this time

62355583
my girlfriend still has flat feet that look like flippers and that will sadly continue to be the case until link moons

62355612
>>62354943
price and chart

62355613
>>62355130
so whats it do that cant be done otherwise?

62355773
>>62355583
You going to buy her new feet?

62355852
>>62355153
what? Like Bentley Mulliner did? Some of the finest automotives ever built are built by coachbuilders who adapted to the 20th century by putting engines inside their carriages

62356084
>>62354943
>>62354945
Page 59. As the document shows, this was announced in November 2025 and is not news and means absolutely nothing for token price because it is specifically regarding price feed data and specifically MESSAGE orchestration between networks, which does absolutely nothing for token price, which doesn't even get into how uncompetitive SWIFT against emergent technologies. This is more cope which is funnily recycled cope which doesn't even know it is recycled by linkies who predictably don't know something that was announced half a year ago despite probably sharing it then too because they just exist to chase "announcement" dopamine never understanding what is being (or pretending to be) announced.

62356144
>>62356084
>was announced
Yeah so? This report is an aggregate of relevant developments and the 2025 news did not show Chainlink in any way. This one, from June 2026, lays it out clearly: it is needed.

>how uncompetitive SWIFT against emerging technologies
Kek there he is. Lemme guess, they are totally going to pick YOUR coin/chain, amirite?

Thats a lotta seethe son, put a tampon on your hole or something

62356149
>>62356084
Did you ask chatgpt to put this together for you like last time?

62356154
>>62356084
>specifically MESSAGE orchestration
kek this is how you know it's a xerpie writing this.
They love pointing out how Chainlink is "only for messages" while XRP is for settlement.

>>62355613
What the fuck is that even supposed to mean?

62356178
>>62355053
>just 3 more years marines! HODL!

62356202
>>62356144
Your post qualified it as a *finally* given confirmation which was itself announced almost a year ago. I am not even arguing for a single coin here, I am just saying the consortium trust model is totally irrelevant now and inferior to automated trust that settles faster and cheaper with substantially less risk. Definitely super organic that within 3 minutes of making my post in this thread in the early morning, I am spam replied to by 3 different IDs none of which even reply to my point. How much is CLL paying brown hands to type nowadays?
>>62356149
Wtf are you talking about? What about what I said was even vaguely conceptually dense or difficult such that you think it would take AI to write or conceive of it? Do you realize how stupid this makes you sound?
>>62356154
I didn't even mention XRP but how does incredulously restating the point I made argue against its validity?

62356205
>>62354943

Dreadfully distinct against the dark

interlinked

A tall white fountain played

62356226
>>62356202
>I didn't even mention XRP
I just explained to you how you outed yourself.

>how does incredulously restating the point I made argue against its validity?
99% of modern banking is "message orchestration". Trying to pretend money isn't made here is just hilarious.

62356241
>>62354976
unironically 2035

62356274
>>62356202
You are the insufferable verbose XRP loser that shits out entire paragraphs of nonsense that noone (SWIFT most of all) cares about except other baggies like yourself. Tell us, is the boom coming up soon? Everytime you say "settlement", "nostro vostro" or "deep liquidity" we have to take a shot.

62356321
>>62356226
>Outted myself
Wut?? This is an insane level of melodrama and headcanonizing instead of just admitting you don't understand the doc's diagram or what it means for the token itself. I never said message orchestration was not a profit center, I said it was not a profit center for token holders. Chainlink Labs will make a clean margin to the extent they are used here in what volume exists to be used for as a business entity. That doesn't mean anything to or for you unless you are a shareholder.
>>62356274
Genuinely don't know what you are talking about or how you think this replies to what I said? I haven't said any of these terms and all you have done is get really mad and try to completely evade the point of the thread. Anything which doesn't just auto-agree despite whether the engine matches the hood advertised is an assault on your identity and proof against the engine's congruity is like unto saying ywnbaw to trannies and must be scorned and repressed as quickly and through as much obvious weird emotional fury as possible!

62356407
>>62356321
You are that XRP guy who writes a lot?

Good luck with ripple dude, I'm sure you will be fine.

62356412
don't you get it stinky
it doesn't matter
value does not accrue to the token
it's all for fat sergey and his harem of fat HR roasties (his personal fetish)
this is how private companies like chainlink labs work. you hold NO legal ability to claim ANY share of these profits because muh chainlink token means NOTHING in the eyes of the law

62356517
>>62356412
>you hold NO legal ability to claim ANY share of these profits
Because that's why people buy crypto right, the profit sharing?
top fucking kek

62356547
>>62356517
value
does
not
accrue
to
token

chainlink could power the whole damn world and the token will still be $7

62356570
>>62356547

62356591

Open thread • Replies: 32 • Updated: 2026-06-12 12:33 UTC

OP

Is it too late to buy some sector specific ETFs to try and ride the wave of this bull market
Obviously no one really knows what will happen, just wish I could have been investing a few years sooner
I already have a large standing in VOO but want to chase growth via sectors
My assumption was to not go into semiconductors or anything based on the computing of AI but things like energy that deal with the infrastructure of AI

Early replies (first 3)

62345926
I've rejected at least 3 east asian women. they're nice girls or whatever but they're not attractive. I exclusively am a latina/brown woman guy, i'm sorry.

62345956
>>62345926

62345961
>>62345819

lol you are about to get heemed

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62345983
>>62345961

62345996
I'm dating a half korean half south american, she's cute, low body count, people pleases the shit out of me (full body massage almost every time I see her) Amazing sex and we're like best friends. Problem is, I want white kids... or I could just be companions with her for the rest of my life and be childless. So I have been going through a crisis in figuring out what to do. Thanks for reading my blog, feel free to roast me or give an opinion

62346078
>>62345926
>>62345996
larp

62346171
>>62345996
my uncle married a chinese woman, they had 2 hapas togethe, the wife was pretty weird and extremely overprotective of the kids (e.g. she wouldn't let them play alone in their fully fenced back yard, even when they were 10+ years old).
They got divorced a few years ago and my aunt moved back to china with her daughter, and my cousin now hates his dad and has trooned out and posts pictures of him wearing dresses on instagram.

62346192
>>62345996
Just have the kids with her. My wife and I are pure white and your mutt daughter can pop out my son's 3/4 white kids on the side while he takes a break from his pure white wife if needed to help restore your bloodline.

62346478
>>62346192
lmao
>>62346171
this is pretty much my nightmare scenario
>>62346078
have sex incel asian girls are easy to colonize

62346501
>>62346478

62346508
>>62345996

62346587

>Blue eyes

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-06-12 13:13 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62355897
good question, the short squeeze will just boost tsla and spacex's valuation to $12T and $50T respectively so it's not that big of a goncern.

62355900
>>62355894
you think he's actually running those companies, he's not. it's all bigger than him a long time ago.

62355910
>>62355894
He doesn't have the best vascular risk profile. Not everything can be found with MRI or cath.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62355914
>>62355900
his cult of personality and promises drive up the valuations of his companies though, no?

62355918
>>62355894
how do we snipe all these billionaires?

like ken griffin haha imagine a more useless billionaire than ken fucking griffin loool

62355952
>>62355894
That'd fail the longtermist roadmap.
They are chasing AI to transfer their consciousness into temporary container until robotics can't provide a full functional artificial body.
And if they can survive decades long space travel then they leave earth before it was unsustainable.
That's the whole X AI - Tesla (robotics rebrand) - Space X story.
If Elon dies the story is failed.

62355972
>>62355894
His living heirs will inherit the stocks and sell everything to waste the money on surgeries, mansions, yachts, buy celebrity, etc. The stocks would then recover to new ath's. They'd find a salesman that can perform better as ceo.

62355981
He's just their lead marketer. Between all his x posts, podcasts and fucking countless women, I don't believe he's got time to actually get shit done. He's more than likely a glowie asset. That being said, it would tank the stock until they replaced him with another controversial sperg.

62356000
60 is about the age you drop dead if you've chronically gotten very low sleep your whole life. Just ask mangaka, all of whom die in their 60s even though Japanese men live to their 80s on average. He's ngmi unironically.

62356013
>>62356000
checked, not to mention the drug use..
wouldn't be like steve jobs handing off company and dying with some dignity
if musk dropped dead before his "time" or before digital immortality how would his cult cope?

62356124
>>62355952
That's a child's fantasy. The level of tech that creates a body for a consciousness means competition is way beyond the human mind. Nothing of Elon Musk remains at that point, no matter how he tries. It's funny that we let these people gamble with the whole planet.

62356240
>>62355914
he's a useless fuck tho - $ for $ invested, he's maybe produced the least actual profit in history. If Elon were to unfortunately shuffle himself off into the Great 2much Elephant Tranq void, TSLA and SPCX might actually end up being ran by someone who can find a profit in all that dross. Altho, some of the shit in SpaceX would be better off taken behind the woodshed. Collection of never-profit fucking garbage.

item.title: It's been over 6 months since we achieved AGI but there still have been no serious policies or discussions on providing… — 8 replies — 4.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
It's been over 6 months since we achieved AGI but there still have been no serious policies or discussions on providing…

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-06-12 12:09 UTC

OP

It's been over 6 months since we achieved AGI but there still have been no serious policies or discussions on providing UBI or profit sharing mechanisms for the massive unemployed workforce we're about to see. If things don't change soon, we're headed towards a mass starvation event and destruction of over half of the world population. We need change now!

Early replies (first 3)

62353458
>>62353056
AGI isnt real

its literally Scifi and will never exist or if it will it wont be for another 250 years.

62353770
>>62353056
>have been no serious policies or discussions on providing UBI or profit sharing mechanisms
Why have discussions, serious or otherwise, about something that isn't gonna happen?

62353774
>>62353458
You're already dead, you just don't realize it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62353781
>>62353056
It's because the play isn't to roll out UBI or help the underclasses at all. The plan is to cull the herd.

62353789
>>62353781
this.

AGI+Robots = total goyim death

the plan is to hunt you down with drones like an animal

62353793
>>62353056
No one is giving you a free blank check because you're dumber than a GPU.

62354901
>>62353793
Being dumber than a GPU is devastating for human culture. Posting bikini vacation pics online already created massive harm, this one alone is going to end society, and it's not even in the top 100 of AI outcomes.

>>62353458
Doesn't matter. It's better doing complex mappings in economic tasks, that is enough.

>>62353056
All societal contracts are the result of threats of violence. What are we going to use to get said UBI?

62355896
Is AI actually getting better or are we just throwing more compute at next word guesser? I don't keep up with developments.

item.title: THe jews in the US are insane — 8 replies — 4.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
THe jews in the US are insane

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-06-12 11:59 UTC

OP

all these freak pedophile tech kike billioniares are absolute sickos

Trump basically handed all of the US tech sector to Jews like Ellison, Musk, Altman who now can legally steal all of the US taxpayer money and the whole US milliatry is now in control of israel, meaning, if US citizens start to revolt, jews will tell Americans to whack their fellow citizens so the jews can contine to lie and steal from the US taxpayer

tech jews, will get all the money and power to create their kike god ai, which is here to lie and steal from people even more.

BUt at least, Musk is now a trillioniare, wow, how cool is our life, we now have the chance of a lifetime to bow down to an autist who fathered x children, of whom 3 or 4 of them are turning from girls into boys or the other way around

But dont hate, you can now bow down to a trillioniare and buy his shitty cars that track everything you do, isnt that great?

You americans are such a bunch of weak jew worshipping faggots

62354832
>>62354824

62354836
